results.title: Single plot can be occupied by multiple renters
results.reasoning: Yes. The report’s Issue 2 identifies the same root flaw: transferToUnoccupiedPlot fails to update toilerState[tokenId].plotId. It points to the same function and code path in LandManager.sol and proposes the same specific fix (setting toilerState[tokenId].plotId = plotId). While the report emphasizes a plot-bricking outcome and the description emphasizes multiple renters/dirty-flag bypass, both exploit the identical missing state update, and fixing it resolves both behaviors.

results.title: Invalid validation in _farmPlots function allowing a malicious user repeated farming without locked funds
results.reasoning: Yes. Audit item #5 explicitly identifies the same off-by-one validation bug in _farmPlots: using `_getNumPlots(landlord) < _toiler.plotId` instead of `<=`, which lets a toiler continue farming on a non-existent plot when the landlord’s number of plots equals the toiler’s plotId. This includes the edge case highlighted in the vulnerability description where plotId = 0 and numPlots = 0 after unlocking funds, allowing continued farming without locked funds. Both reference the same code path and check, have the same impact (unbacked rewards), and share the same fix (change < to <=). Fixing the report’s issue would fix the described vulnerability.

results.title: Miscalculation in _farmPlots function could lead to a user unable to unstake all NFTs
results.reasoning: Yes. The report’s findings #4 and #11 describe the same bug in LandManager._farmPlots: the reward adjustment applies (S + S*finalBonus)/100 (i.e., S*(1+B)/100) instead of S + (S*B)/100, causing negative results for negative finalBonus that get cast to uint256 and wrap. The report also notes this can revert during subsequent tax computation and, because unstakeMunchable is force-coupled to _farmPlots (see #9), can lock NFTs. It references the same code path and proposes the same fix (apply /100 only to the bonus term). Fixing the reported issue would fix the described vulnerability.

results.title: in farmPlots() an underflow in edge case leading to freeze of funds (NFT)
results.reasoning: Yes. Audit item 3 (“Timestamp Underflow in Dirty Flag Path Permanently Locks NFTs in Contract”) describes the same bug: in _farmPlots, when a toiler’s plot becomes invalid the code sets timestamp = plotMetadata[landlord].lastUpdated and later computes (timestamp - _toiler.lastToilDate), which can underflow and revert, causing unstake/farm to be permanently blocked due to forceFarmPlots. The vulnerability description presents the PRICE_PER_PLOT increase as the trigger, whereas the audit mentions plot count decreases via unlocking, but both exploit the same code path and arithmetic underflow mechanism. A single fix (ensure timestamp >= lastToilDate or otherwise guard the subtraction/choose correct timestamp) would resolve both scenarios.

results.title: Failure to update dirty flag in transferToUnoccupiedPlot prevents reward accumulation on valid plot
results.reasoning: Yes. Audit item #7 ('Dirty-Flagged Tokens Stuck in Zero-Yield Dead State with No Recovery') identifies that once a token’s dirty flag is set, _farmPlots skips it and there is no mechanism to clear the flag; it also states the intended recovery function transferToUnoccupiedPlot is ineffective. This matches H-05’s root cause: transferToUnoccupiedPlot does not reset the dirty flag, so the token continues to be skipped in _farmPlots and earns no rewards even after moving to a valid plot. The code path (_farmPlots’ dirty check and transferToUnoccupiedPlot) and fix (reset dirty, e.g., in transferToUnoccupiedPlot and update lastToilDate) are the same, so fixing the report’s issue would fix H-05.
